results.title: GitHub Actions
results.description: GitHub Actions allows you to automate, customize, and execute your software development workflows directly in your repository, enabling CI/CD and customized automation through actions.

results.title: GitHub Actions
results.description: GitHub Actions automates, customizes, and executes software development workflows directly in your repository, allowing you to discover, create, and share actions for tasks like CI/CD.

results.title: RunsOn Action
results.description: A GitHub Action that integrates with RunsOn for features like magic caching, cost analysis, performance metrics, and sccache configuration.

results.title: GitHub Release Action
results.description: A GitHub Action for creating GitHub Releases on Linux, Windows, and macOS virtual environments, allowing for customization of release assets and notes.

results.title: RunsOn
results.description: RunsOn is a service that allows you to run GitHub Actions on your own AWS infrastructure, offering a cheaper, faster, and simpler alternative to traditional runners.

results.title: GitHub Actions Runner
results.description: The GitHub Actions Runner is the application that executes jobs from a GitHub Actions workflow, usable in hosted virtual environments or self-hosted setups.

results.title: Skills GitHub Action
results.description: A GitHub Action for testing and triggering workflows, demonstrating GitHub Actions automation capabilities through workflow execution.

results.title: Run Gemini CLI
results.description: A GitHub Action that integrates the Gemini CLI into your development workflow for autonomous coding tasks and on-demand collaboration on GitHub.

results.title: RunsOn
results.description: RunsOn provides self-hosted, scalable, and cost-effective GitHub Actions runners in your AWS account, offering faster builds and significant CI spend reduction.

results.title: GitHub Runner EC2 Manager
results.description: A command-line tool and GitHub Action to create and terminate EC2 instances for GitHub Actions runners, supporting both on-demand and spot instances.

results.title: Google GitHub Actions Deploy CloudRun
results.description: The deploy-cloudrun GitHub Action deploys containers to Google Cloud Run from source or a container image, making the service URL available as an output.

results.title: Pulumi GitHub Actions
results.description: Pulumi GitHub Actions lets you run Pulumi operations from GitHub Actions workflows to preview, validate, update, or destroy cloud infrastructure and applications, manage stacks and outputs, comment on PRs, and integrate with Pulumi backends and secret providers.

results.title: Release Please Action
results.description: Automates releases for GitHub repositories using Conventional Commit messages, creating release pull requests based on commit history.

results.title: GitHub Action Renovate
results.description: A GitHub Action designed to run Renovate in a self-hosted environment, enabling automated dependency updates for repositories.

results.title: VirusTotal GitHub Action
results.description: A GitHub Action to upload and scan files with VirusTotal, including local files, release assets, and through VirusTotal Monitor.

results.title: SSH Action
results.description: SSH for GitHub Actions is a GitHub Action for executing remote SSH commands securely and easily in CI/CD workflows, supporting multi-host, proxy, and advanced authentication.

results.title: Replicated Actions
results.description: Replicated Actions are GitHub Actions that enable deployment, testing, and compatibility matrix validation for applications using the Replicated platform.

results.title: GitHub Actions for VS Code
results.description: A Visual Studio Code extension that enables users to manage GitHub Actions workflows, view run history, and author workflows with syntax highlighting, validation, and code completion.

results.title: Cypress IO GitHub Action
results.description: Cypress IO GitHub Action is a GitHub Action that runs Cypress End-to-End or Component tests in CI workflows, offering features like dependency installation, caching, and optional recording to Cypress Cloud.

results.title: GitHub Actions Checkout
results.description: This GitHub Action checks out your repository under $GITHUB_WORKSPACE, allowing your workflow to access it with options for fetching history, persisting credentials, and falling back to the REST API.

results.title: Laravel Lang Actions
results.description: Laravel Lang Actions provides a set of GitHub Actions to automate the management and synchronization of language files for Laravel projects.

results.title: ubc GitHub Action
results.description: A GitHub Action that downloads and installs the ubc CLI tool, adds it to PATH, and executes ubc commands with support for caching and license configuration.

results.title: GitHub Script
results.description: This action simplifies writing scripts in workflows that utilize the GitHub API and workflow run context, providing access to octokit, context, core, glob, io, exec, and require.

results.title: Testflows GitHub Hetzner Runners
results.description: Autoscaling Self-Hosted GitHub Actions Runners on Hetzner Cloud.

results.title: Expo GitHub Action
results.description: Expo GitHub Action makes it easy to automate EAS builds or updates

results.title: Atlas GitHub Action
results.description: The official GitHub Action for the MongoDB Atlas CLI, enabling users to run Atlas CLI commands within GitHub workflows for managing MongoDB Atlas resources.

results.title: GitHub Actions Deploy AUR
results.description: GitHub Actions to publish AUR package

results.title: GitHub Toolkit
results.description: The GitHub Actions Toolkit provides a set of packages to make creating actions easier, offering utilities for core functionalities, execution, globbing, HTTP requests, I/O, tool caching, and more.

results.title: Terraform and OpenTofu GitHub Actions
results.description: A suite of GitHub Actions for automating Terraform and OpenTofu workflows, enabling effective Infrastructure as Code management.

results.title: act
results.description: act allows you to run GitHub Actions locally, enabling fast feedback on workflow changes and serving as a local task runner without needing to commit and push.
